title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
authors: Patrick Lewis, Ethan Perez, Aleksandra Piktus, et al.
summary: Introduces retrieval-augmented generation, combining parametric generation with retrieved non-parametric memory for knowledge-intensive tasks.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
authors: Shunyu Yao, Jeffrey Zhao, Dian Yu, et al.
summary: Shows how language models can interleave reasoning traces with tool actions for more grounded task solving.

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
authors: Noah Shinn, Federico Cassano, Ashwin Gopinath, et al.
summary: Presents a reflection loop for agents that critique prior attempts and improve subsequent actions.

title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
authors: Aman Madaan, Niket Tandon, Prakhar Gupta, et al.
summary: Demonstrates iterative self-feedback and refinement for improving generated outputs without extra training.

title: RAGAS: Automated Evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: Shahul Es, Jithin James, Luis Espinosa-Anke, Steven Schockaert
summary: Defines evaluation dimensions for retrieval-augmented generation including faithfulness, answer relevance, and context relevance.

title: Survey of Hallucination in Natural Language Generation
authors: Ziwei Ji, Nayeon Lee, Rita Frieske, et al.
summary: Surveys hallucination definitions, causes, and mitigation methods across natural language generation systems.

title: Large Language Models Encode Clinical Knowledge
authors: Karan Singhal, Shekoofeh Azizi, Tao Tu, et al.
summary: Evaluates large language models on medical question answering and discusses opportunities and limitations for medicine.

title: An Overview of Clinical Decision Support Systems: Benefits, Risks, and Strategies for Success
authors: Reed T. Sutton, David Pincock, Daniel C. Baumgart, et al.
summary: Reviews clinical decision support benefits, implementation risks, human factors, alert fatigue, and governance requirements.

title: Effects of Computerized Clinical Decision Support Systems on Practitioner Performance and Patient Outcomes
authors: Amit X. Garg, Neill K. J. Adhikari, Heather McDonald, et al.
summary: Systematic review of computerized clinical decision support effects on practitioner performance and patient outcomes.

title: Improving Clinical Practice Using Clinical Decision Support Systems: A Systematic Review of Trials
authors: Kensaku Kawamoto, Caitlin A. Houlihan, E. Andrew Balas, David F. Lobach
summary: Identifies features associated with effective clinical decision support interventions.

title: CONSORT-AI Extension: Reporting Guidelines for Clinical Trials of Artificial Intelligence Interventions
authors: Xiaoxuan Liu, Samantha Cruz Rivera, David Moher, et al.
summary: Provides reporting standards for clinical trials evaluating AI interventions.

title: SPIRIT-AI Extension: Protocol Guidelines for Clinical Trials of Artificial Intelligence Interventions
authors: Samantha Cruz Rivera, Xiaoxuan Liu, An-Wen Chan, et al.
summary: Defines protocol reporting items for clinical trials involving AI interventions.

title: DECIDE-AI: New Reporting Guidelines to Bridge the Development-to-Implementation Gap in Clinical Artificial Intelligence
authors: Brennan Vasey, Xiaoxuan Liu, et al.
summary: Offers early-stage reporting guidance for clinical AI systems before full clinical trials.

title: Ethics and Governance of Artificial Intelligence for Health
authors: World Health Organization
summary: WHO guidance on ethical, legal, governance, and human-rights issues for AI in health.

title: On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?
authors: Emily M. Bender, Timnit Gebru, Angelina McMillan-Major, Shmargaret Shmitchell
summary: Discusses risks of large language models including bias, environmental cost, and misleading fluent text.

title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
authors: Qingyun Wu, Gagan Bansal, Jieyu Zhang, et al.
summary: Introduces AutoGen, a framework for building LLM applications through multi-agent conversation.

title: LangGraph Graph API Documentation
authors: LangChain
summary: Official documentation for LangGraph state graphs, nodes, edges, conditional routing, and workflow control.

title: Agent Development Kit Multi-Agent Systems Documentation
authors: Google
summary: Google ADK documentation for composing multiple agents into multi-agent systems.

title: CrewAI Documentation
authors: CrewAI
summary: Framework documentation for role-based agents, crews, tools, memory, and workflows.

title: Semantic Kernel Agent Framework Documentation
authors: Microsoft
summary: Microsoft documentation for agent abstractions and collaboration patterns in Semantic Kernel.

title: LLM Powered Autonomous Agents
authors: Lilian Weng
summary: Technical overview of planning, memory, and tool use in autonomous LLM agents.
